22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}

36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}

50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}

64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}

125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}

147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}

54	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
55		f.mu.Lock()
56		defer f.mu.Unlock()
57		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
58			if f.wtHeadRevErr != nil {
59				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
60			}
61			return f.wtHeadRev, nil
62		}
63		if len(f.headRevSeq) > 0 {
64			idx := f.headRevIdx
65			if idx >= len(f.headRevSeq) {
66				idx = len(f.headRevSeq) - 1
67			}
68			rev := f.headRevSeq[idx]
69			f.headRevIdx++
70			return rev, nil
71		}
72		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
73	}

206	// fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit returns a LandingAdvancer callback that maps the
207	// fake git's Merge/UpdateRef semantics onto the coordinator-pattern advancer
208	// interface. Semantically, "mergeCalls" now means "number of times
209	// LandBeadResult invoked the advancer (attempted to advance the target
210	// branch)". Used by tests that were written against the old Merge() path so
211	// they continue to pass after the land-coordinator refactor.
212	func fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git *fakeExecuteBeadGit) func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
213		return func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
214			if err := git.Merge("", res.ResultRev); err != nil {
215				preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
216				// Record the preserve ref in the fake's refs map so tests that
217				// assert git.refs[preserveRef] continue to pass.
218				_ = git.UpdateRef("", preserveRef, res.ResultRev)
219				return &agent.LandResult{
220					Status:      "preserved",
221					PreserveRef: preserveRef,
222					Reason:      "merge failed",
223				}, nil
224			}
225			return &agent.LandResult{Status: "landed", NewTip: res.ResultRev}, nil
226		}
227	}
